# Lunogram

> Lunogram is a self-hosted, open-source multi-channel marketing automation platform for SaaS companies. It enables targeted customer communication across email, SMS, push notifications, and webhooks through a visual journey builder and dynamic user segmentation.

Lunogram is a continuation of Parcelvoy, an archived marketing automation platform. The project is being rebuilt from the ground up with a modern architecture, transitioning from Node.js/MySQL to Go/PostgreSQL for improved performance and maintainability.

Key characteristics:
- Self-hosted with no vendor lock-in
- Multi-channel: Email, SMS, Push Notifications, Webhooks
- Visual journey builder for customer communication automation
- Dynamic user segmentation with rule-based lists
- Organization/B2B management capabilities
- Open source and community-driven
- Alternative to enterprise tools like Iterable and Braze

## Core Features

- **Multi-Channel Messaging**: Send communications via Email, SMS, Push Notifications, and Webhooks from a single platform
- **Visual Journey Builder**: Drag-and-drop workflow editor with step types including Entrance, Delay, Gate, Email, Push, SMS, Webhook, and Exit
- **Dynamic Segmentation**: Create rule-based user lists that automatically update based on user properties, events, and organization attributes
- **Organization Management**: B2B-ready with customer organization modeling for account-based marketing
- **A/B Testing**: Variant campaigns and journey branches for optimization
- **Multi-Locale Campaigns**: Timezone-aware delivery with multiple language support
- **SSO & RBAC**: SAML/OpenID support with role-based access control

## Integrations

Lunogram integrates with popular email and SMS providers:
- **Email**: Resend, SendGrid, Amazon SES, Mailgun, SMTP
- **SMS**: Twilio, Plivo, Vonage, Telnyx
- **Push**: Apple Push Notification service (APN), Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM)

## Pricing

- **Self-Hosted (Free)**: Unlimited users, messages, journeys, and team members with full source code access
- **Cloud Starter ($49/month)**: Up to 10,000 users, 50,000 messages/month, managed infrastructure
- **Cloud Pro ($199/month)**: Up to 100,000 users, 500,000 messages/month, priority support, SSO
- **Enterprise (Custom)**: Custom limits, dedicated support, SLA guarantee

## Pages

- [Home](https://lunogram.dev): Landing page with interactive demos showcasing the journey builder and segmentation features
- [About](https://lunogram.dev/about): Company mission, team information, and origin story explaining the Parcelvoy fork
- [Pricing](https://lunogram.dev/pricing): Detailed pricing tiers with feature comparison and FAQ
- [Blog](https://lunogram.dev/blog): Updates and insights from the Lunogram team

## Resources

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/lunogram/platform): Source code for the Lunogram platform
- [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/lunogram/platform/discussions): Community discussion forum
- [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/lunogram/platform/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): How to contribute to the project

## Technical Details

The platform is built with:
- **Go 1.25+** backend with a modular architecture
- **PostgreSQL** for data persistence (separate databases for management, users, and journeys)
- **Redis** for caching and session management
- **NATS** for pub/sub messaging between services
- **WASM plugin system** for extensible provider integrations (built with TinyGo)

### WASM Plugin System

Providers are implemented as sandboxed WASM modules for security and portability. Each provider implements:
- `manifest()` - Returns metadata and configuration schema
- `send()` - Sends messages via the provider's API

Built-in providers include Logger (debug), Resend (email), and Twilio (email/SMS).

### Deployment

Lunogram runs via Docker Compose with a single command:
```
docker compose up -d
```

Default access at http://localhost:8080 with basic auth credentials.
